b:T1a3fa,<div class="specification">
<p>DNA forms chromosomes, but much of eukaryote DNA is non-coding.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Pixabay.]</p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>People can be analysed genetically for risks of specific diseases before they actually develop. One of these is type I diabetes.</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe the structure of nucleosomes.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain how the two strands of the DNA double helix are held together.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>There are multiple tandem repeats of nucleotide segments of DNA found in the non-coding DNA between genes. Outline how tandem repeats are used for DNA profiling.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.iii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain what happens in a person when they develop type I diabetes.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State how type I diabetes should be treated to avoid harmful health consequences of the condition.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div>c:Tb8d,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a unit of DNA wound/coiled around 8 histone proteins / octamer;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>hydrogen bonding <span style="text-decoration:underline;">between</span> nucleotides / bases;</li>
<li>complementary base pairs;</li>
<li>adenine-thymine and cytosine-guanine form base pairs (between the two strands with H-bonding);</li>
<li>2 bonds between A and T, while 3 bonds between C and G;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>OWTTE</em></p>
<p><em>Full names required for c and d though use ecf.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>tandem repeats (at one locus) vary in number of times sequence repeats / represent different alleles for one locus;</li>
<li>DNA sample cut by restriction enzymes into fragments;</li>
<li>samples of DNA are amplified at specific genetic sites with PCR;</li>
<li>the fragments are separated by their size/number of repeats with gel electrophoresis;</li>
<li>fluorescent/radioactive label attached to different tandem repeats;</li>
<li>data from several loci at one time uniquely identify individuals / like a fingerprint, combinations of alleles are specific to an individual;</li>
<li>
<span style="text-decoration:underline;">comparisons</span>/<span style="text-decoration:underline;">similarities</span> between fragment patterns to determine paternity/evidence match to a suspect’s profile / other example of comparison/similarity;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>OWTTE</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.iii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>insulin is necessary to control/regulate blood glucose concentrations<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>insulin is necessary for the cells to take up glucose (for energy);</li>
<li>insufficient insulin is made by the pancreas<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/><span style="text-decoration:underline;">autoimmune response</span>/<span style="text-decoration:underline;">antibodies</span> destroy the (β) cells of the pancreas that make insulin;</li>
<li>reduced uptake of glucose from the blood / glucose accumulates in the blood / elevated blood glucose levels;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>“sugar” is NOT accepted in place of glucose. However, this should only be penalized once; i.e., utiltize ECF.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>inject insulin / monitoring blood glucose / devices that release insulin;</li>
<li>decrease consumption of sugars/CHO / diet modification;</li>
<li>increase exercise;</li>
<li>keep weight in healthy range;</li>
</ol>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div>d:T4b7,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>The structure of a nucleosome was correctly described for many, but some did not mention the number of histones.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
[N/A]
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>There was a noticeable number of blank answers with respect to this question about using tandem repeats as the foundation of DNA profiling. Others had a good idea about PCR and gel electrophoresis, but many didn't mention restriction enzymes and did not clearly understand that patterns are compared.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.iii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>This question about blood sugar regulation was well done by most. This was a question where the use of general terms rather than specific terms led to lost marks. For example, sugars instead of glucose and ‘the body’ instead of ‘the pancreas’</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
[N/A]
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/7/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","7"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.2.HL.TZ2.5"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","7-1-dna-structure-and-replication, 2-6-structure-of-dna-and-rna, 6-6-hormones-homeostasis-and-reproduction"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$d"}}]]}]]}]
